name: Larry Ridley
abstract: Laurence “Larry” Howard Ridley II (born September 3, 1937) is an American jazz bassist and music educator. He is an inductee of the Indianapolis Jazz Hall of Fame.

interviews.title: Larry Ridley interviewed by Monk Rowe, Toronto, Canada, January 10, 2003

about_quotes.speaker: David N. Baker
about_quotes.evidence: I’ve tried to be pretty much [of] a mentor to Larry... He has studied with me from time to time... We’re in a quintet which consisted of Joe Kennedy and myself... Larry Ridley...
about_quotes.doc_title: David N. Baker — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Jimmy Wormworth
about_quotes.evidence: I’ve had an experience with Larry Ridley, who I worked with, and Charles Davis as well, they both made CDs in the past two or three years on their own.
about_quotes.doc_title: Jimmy Wormworth interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, January 6, 2002

about_quotes.speaker: Tiny Grimes
about_quotes.evidence: Now for a while I was in his little group, he had a five-piece group. It was, let's see Larry Ridley, Howard, he played the piano, and myself.

about_quotes.speaker: Frank Foster
about_quotes.evidence: At Davidson College of Rutgers University I was one of the jazz professors under Larry Ridley
about_quotes.doc_title: Frank Foster — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: David Weiss
about_quotes.evidence: Larry Ridley did it. And when Mabern couldn’t do it we decided I could put together some more Freddie Hubbard alumni. Ronnie Mathews did it and Kiane Zawadi did it. And that concert was a success.
about_quotes.doc_title: David Weiss interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, May 3, 2022

about_quotes.speaker: Dan Morgenstern
about_quotes.evidence: Larry Ridley, whom I had known for a long time before coming to Rutgers and who certainly should get credit for being one of the people who was involved in getting me here
about_quotes.doc_title: Dan Morgenstern — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History
